sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Freight stocks lead Jacksonville market in overall down quarter
feed.summary: Despite a weak freight market and an overall down quarter for stocks, Jacksonville-based freight haulers CSX Corp. and Landstar System Inc. both saw significant gains in the first quarter of 2026, rising 13% and 12% respectively. This performance was attributed by a Morgan Stanley analyst to transportation stocks being seen as a defensive play in a volatile market. The article also covers updates on other Jacksonville companies, including Rayonier Advanced Materials, FRP Holdings, Fortegra Group, and Tapestry Inc., along with Forbes' list inclusions for Shad Khan and Wayne Weaver.

feed.title: Amazon Found a Quiet New Way to Squeeze Sellers Without Raising a Single Fee
feed.summary: Amazon sellers are protesting a new 3.5% fuel surcharge, but the author argues that a more significant change is Amazon's decision to stop allowing some sellers to pay for advertising with credit cards. This change forces sellers to have ad costs deducted directly from earnings, eliminating their ability to float expenses and earn credit card rewards, thereby significantly impacting their cash flow and margins.

feed.title: EQT Corporation 2026 Annual Meeting Results: Shareholder Approval of Executive Compensation, LTIP Amendment, and Auditor Ratification
feed.summary: EQT Corporation held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders, where key decisions were made regarding executive compensation, amendments to the Long-Term Incentive Plan (LTIP), and auditor ratification. Shareholders re-elected all nominated directors and approved the "Say-on-Pay" for named executive officers for 2025. The third amendment to the 2020 LTIP was also approved, allowing for greater flexibility in equity-based awards, which could impact future share issuance and compensation costs.

feed.title: Raymond James upgrades Equitable Holdings stock on merger outlook By Investing.com
feed.summary: Raymond James has upgraded Equitable Holdings (NYSE: EQH) to Strong Buy with a $58 price target, citing a favorable outlook for its merger with Corebridge Financial. Despite a recent 11% year-to-date stock decline, the firm sees an opportunity, noting management's share buybacks and consistent dividend increases.

feed.title: Winners And Losers Of Q4: Yum China (NYSE:YUMC) Vs The Rest Of The Traditional Fast Food Stocks

feed.title: HPE Deepens NVIDIA Alliance As AI Cloud Push Shapes Valuation Story
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has strengthened its partnership with NVIDIA to accelerate enterprise AI adoption, introducing an upgraded HPE Private Cloud AI platform with enhanced performance and security. This strategic move aims to meet the demand for scalable and compliant AI deployment, especially in regulated sectors, by integrating AI workloads more closely with HPE's existing offerings. For investors, this highlights management's response to AI demand, though the company's high debt and dividend coverage are important considerations.

feed.title: State Street Group Ceases to Be Substantial Holder in Deterra Royalties
feed.summary: State Street Corporation and its asset management subsidiaries are no longer substantial holders in Deterra Royalties Ltd as of April 14, 2026. This change signifies a reduction in their collective voting stake below the substantial holding threshold, potentially reshaping Deterra’s institutional investor base and influencing trading liquidity. Deterra Royalties is a resources royalty company that earns income from mining and natural resources projects.

feed.title: Digitization in the Logistics Supply Chain Market Analysis By Key Players SAP SE, IBM ,etc
feed.summary: The digitization in the logistics supply chain market is projected to grow significantly from US$ 17.63 billion in 2023 to US$ 34.85 billion by 2031, driven by the expansion of retail and e-commerce, and the need for efficient supply chain management. Key drivers include the surge in e-commerce demand and the global push for automation, while government initiatives and a focus on sustainability present further opportunities. North America currently leads the market, with Asia-Pacific expected to see rapid growth due to industrialization and government support.
